content: Just as language learners build long-term writing proficiency by iterative, explicit reflection on their output in high school English writing workshops, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by integrating structured, graded reflexion exercises that mimic how human learners incrementally refine their reasoning over repeated practice cycles. This framing turns abstract LLM self-reflection into a teachable, curriculum-style skill rather than a one-off prompt engineering trick.
